author: @timeofthenick
text:
I appreciate this video a lot (was literally just looking for a follow up on 1056 which I'm sure I'll get to in one way or another) since you've said time and time again that you're desire is to instill a curiosity, an interest to try things... I call it tinkering.

I truly value your work sir and love watching your videos old and new alike (especially when I know the thing I was looking for was in "one" of them) and always swap over to see what newest idea you have whenever you post now.

author: @poseidon201
text: Good points and i take them all on board, and understand the business model. I can only speak for myself when if like the windturbine I've followed all the videos and shared your enthusiasm through the journey, it can be disappointing not to see the final outcome. I love all your videos and can't wait to see them. Perhaps there is some way of viewers voting on your chosen projects which get continued, with maybe a crowd funding page, so you can choose your projects, keep the advertisers happy, and keep the majority of your viewers on board?? Keep up the good work.

author: @ooslum
text:
Years ago Morrisons had a bad year, 6% down and announced lots of changes. To me a Morrisons shopper it felt they cared more about the lost 6% than the 94% they had, I stopped shopping there even though they have now reversed those changes. This is not a criticism, just a feeling that your not a Primark channel, more of an M&S. 
I have noticed a shift recently to a lot more short info videos but maybe bigger projects should  be longer with less iterative steps and more conclusive end. Sorry I don't know the answer.

author: @PVflying
text: I think you would find there’s huge interest in VAWTs and the time taken to set up an already built turbine to a load and measure performance in a wind is actually very small compared to the time you put into new builds. I can’t help thinking your stated reason isn’t really the full story.

replies.author: @gizmonicman9879
replies.text: I have to agree with MrCurry here. Not following through with something you spent a fair amount of your time on (and I spent some bit of time watching the evolution of) makes it seem that your results weren't something that you wanted to share. You need to show the conclusion of these multi-episode projects, warts and all, even if they don't return the results you expect. To do less than this makes me wonder if you're really being square with all of your viewers.

author: @jimmycorkhill1390
text: There are lots of youtube channels that dont get paid for any content, adverts or subscribers and they get heavily censored. Your related words are incorrect. Youtube is part of the ever increasing totalitarianism, you may not see it, you may ignore it, but that doesnt mean it isnt real. I anticipate that this is not the sort of comment you like to read, but honesty is to me the best form of kindness. Best wishes.

author: @andygoodyear5146
text: In my humble opinion this channel has always been about ideas and inspiration, not necessarily finished projects. It is up to individual viewers to seize a concept that particularly interests them and take it further.  Looked at in that way your channel is a massive success and I, for one, thank you for that. Keep up the good work, Rob.

replies.author: @jaredcarlson3500
replies.text: Agreed. If people expect king of random style entertainment, then they will be frustrated. Rob does what few people do these days. Instruct in a fashion that inspires vast amounts of individual research and creativity. Thanks Rob for your impact in the world!

author: @nazgullinux6601
text:
In order to get DC motors, you gotta look in the surplus industrial market. Lots of very powerful, but outdated or end-of-life, equipment can be obtained for pennies on the dollar. Check Jeremy Fielding for info on this kind of thing. He built a CNC Plasma table from scavenged industrial equipment. Many he got for near-free just by speaking with business owners.

You can't just look in the retail markets laddie.

author: @chuxxsss

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text:
sounds like you are saying on the advertisers, there needs to be a showman that generates constant enthusiasm, and if you or the others presenting can't do this, (it looks ridiculous, when I see other YTers doing it, so glad you guys are more down to earth, and not acting a fool as it takes a very good actor to pull it off with out looking cringy and fake) 

So as I was saying if you are not going to be the showman, then its down to the project's aspects to be the showman, or basically another way is "the project has to be able to just sell itself because I am not a sales man here to get people to buy it (with their attention and interest) that don't want it in the first place. 

Instead you are saying you are the type of salesman that your enthusiasm has to be genuine or its not there, and you recognize projects that are interesting and sell themselves, :) 

Personally the aspect of a project that is interesting and useful is often the only part that matters in the end. as that is the part that teaches people something new. :)

author: @czechyorker
text: I am also not a rich man. Obviously one day I may stop supporting this channel if I can't afford it. For now, I feel that this is the money well spent. Just cut it somewhere else if you can as I did.

author: @rastamanralph6670
text:
Unless you have a YouTube channel you don't understand what goes into it. I don't have to edit and spend that much on equipment other than what I've bought for myself to make better videos but that's because I make videos of my interests and hope people like them.
I haven't got a big channel so there's not much demand for people wanting to see certain things from me and feel for the people who do and who spend a lot of precious time doing everything that goes into it. Time is actually my biggest problem because I'm not usually well enough to do finding gold videos so have to settle with random things that I'm doing there and then. Some of the videos I've got high views on are not what I would of thought would be good and happens over time.
To be honest I'll never make any money from it but do love to read and reply to the comments. 👊💛👍

author: @martinjandijkstra3205

author: @C-M-E
text: What drew me to this channel originally was because it popped up in a search about sorel cements. What kept me here was your seemingly random but varied pool of experiments. What I aim to do eventually is focus on one big project, but as does happen, you discover about 6 really big side effects and even more little ones that could be really useful independent processes in other applications. That's what I'd like to see you guys run with to make some side pay. You're such good innovators and have a wealth of incubation projects that I'm sure would be of financial benefit to any number of companies out there that may want to run with a process or adapt it to work on whatever they're doing now.

author: @nigeljohnson9820

author: @McRootbeer
text: Sucks that not everyone has the interest to finish a video series.  I'm still hoping to see the performance of the improved hho generator, and it would be awesome to see you welding something useful with it.  All the other videos I've seen on hho welding is either jewelers or some home experimenter cutting holes in aluminum cans.

author: @MikesAllotment
text: Thanks for explaining that Rob - very useful to get an insight into what 'drives' the channel.   I subscribe to YouTube channels because a) I'm interested in the content and b) I like the way the channel is presented - hence I subscribe to your channel along with many others.  It is however slightly annoying when you follow a really good series of videos ('Bessie' being a good example), only to then find that you have 'abandoned' it due to an apparent lack of interest (i.e. "the line has been drawn" as you put it).  Maybe it would be worth you asking your subscribers whether they would be interested in you continuing with a particular project and asking them to reply one way or the other in the comments.  That way you will know for sure whether your subscribers want you to abandon a project or not, and also has the added benefit of you getting a heap-load of comments (which I understand is also beneficial for the channel's ££).  Just a thought.   Keep up the good work - it's very much appreciated.

author: @karlmyers6518
